title: Adjustments to draft financial statements
summary: 9706 P2 — accruals, prepayments, depreciation, irrecoverable debts, and inventory.
learningObjectives: Provide for irrecoverable debts and allowances.
Value closing inventory and correct errors.

simpleExplanation.title: Fine-tuning the draft accounts
simpleExplanation.summary: Year-end adjustments match costs and income to the correct period — accruals, prepayments, depreciation, and bad debts before final statements.
steps: Accrual — expense owed, not yet paid.
Irrecoverable debts — write off and allowance.

flashcards.back: DR Expense, CR Accrual (liability) — owed but unpaid at year-end.
DR Prepayment (asset), CR Expense — paid early, benefit next period.
DR Accrued income (asset), CR Income — earned but not yet received.
DR Depreciation expense, CR Accumulated depreciation.
DR Irrecoverable debts expense, CR Trade receivable.
Increase: DR Allowance expense, CR Allowance account.

sections.content: **Adjustments** (1.5.1) — list **each adjustment with journal** before drafting SPL/SOFP. Examiners award method marks for clear accrual/prepayment logic.

sections.question: Draft rent expense in trial balance $12,000. Rent of $1,200 for January next year was paid in December. Electricity $800 owed at year-end. Depreciation on equipment $3,500. Calculate adjusted rent, electricity, and total expense increase.
sections.solution:
**Rent prepayment:** $1,200 paid for next period → **reduce** rent expense.
Adjusted rent = 12,000 − 1,200 = **$10,800**

**Electricity accrual:** $800 owed → **increase** expense.
Adjusted electricity = **$800** (if nil in TB) or add to existing balance.

**Depreciation:** **$3,500** charge to SPL.

**Journals:**
DR Prepayments 1,200 / CR Rent 1,200
DR Electricity 800 / CR Accruals 800
DR Depreciation 3,500 / CR Acc. dep. 3,500

**Total extra expense** (net of prepayment) = 800 + 3,500 − 1,200 = **$3,100** (plus any electricity already in TB).
